options.A: Two single loops thrown in opposite direction
options.B: Two double loops thrown in opposite direction
options.C: Two single loops thrown in same direction
options.D: Two double loops thrown in Same direction

history.message:
Agent1 Round 1:  The square knot, also known as the reef knot or figure-eight knot, involves two separate overhand knots tied one on top of another with each half being thrown in an alternating manner. This creates secure binding that can be easily untied when necessary without coming undone under tension like other types of knots might do.

The correct option is A: Two single loops thrown in opposite direction, which accurately describes how individual strands from opposing directions are looped together sequentially to form this reliable yet easy-to-release knot used frequently for bandages among others where securing but not necessarily permanently tying off tissue is needed during surgical procedures. **Answer Choice:** A
Answer: A
